meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS pricing to Solve no understanding of APIs (Advanced Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no understanding of APIs. A Advanced-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: no understanding of APIs

content.seo_title: Freelancer SaaS Pricing Guide: Solving API Integration Hurdles
content.seo_description: Master SaaS pricing and bypass API complexity. A premium case study for freelancers using Antigravity and Claude Code to accelerate development cycles.
content.answer_block: Stop letting API complexity kill your project velocity. By leveraging Antigravity to abstract integration logic and using Claude Code for autonomous feature generation, you can implement robust SaaS pricing models—like tiered subscriptions or usage-based billing—without needing to manually architect complex API handshake protocols or backend middleware.
content.intro:
The clock is ticking on your client's project, and you are staring at a wall of documentation for a Stripe API integration that feels like ancient hieroglyphics. You are a builder, not a protocol engineer, and the technical debt of 'not knowing the API' is threatening to turn a high-margin project into a bottomless pit of billable hours.

But what if you didn't have to parse the documentation at all? By shifting your workflow to an AI-first stack, you can bypass the manual drudgery of API orchestration. This case study demonstrates how to use Antigravity and Claude Code to wrap complex SaaS pricing logic into a clean, deployable service, allowing you to ship professional-grade features while you focus on the UI and the client's business value.

steps.title: Architecting the Pricing Schema
steps.description: Define your SaaS pricing tiers in a structured format so the AI understands the business logic before touching any code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze these three SaaS pricing tiers (Starter, Pro, Enterprise) and generate a JSON schema that maps them to a Stripe-compatible billing structure.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Create a TypeScript interface for our pricing schema based on the Antigravity output.'

steps.title: Abstracting API Complexity
steps.description: Use Antigravity to create a wrapper that simplifies the Stripe API calls into simple, human-readable functions for your frontend.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an abstraction layer for Stripe subscriptions that exposes only three functions: createCustomer, subscribeToTier, and cancelSubscription.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write --file src/lib/billing.ts --content 'Use the abstracted functions to build a secure backend controller.'

steps.title: Implementing Webhooks with Claude Code
steps.description: Don't manually handle API events; generate a robust webhook listener that automatically updates your database state.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a secure webhook handler that listens for invoice.payment_succeeded and updates the user's 'is_active' status in Supabase.

steps.title: Testing the Checkout Flow
steps.description: Simulate the end-to-end checkout experience to ensure the API integration is fault-tolerant.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a suite of integration tests for the pricing flow, covering successful payment, failed payment, and subscription upgrades.

steps.title: Deploying the Billing Service
steps.description: Finalize the infrastructure and deploy the service, ensuring all API keys are handled via environment variables.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a deployment configuration for a serverless billing function that uses environment variables for Stripe and database secrets.

pro_tips: Always use a mock-api layer in development to avoid hitting rate limits while testing your pricing logic.
Use Claude Code to document your API wrappers automatically; it saves hours of manual readme maintenance.
Implement 'idempotency keys' for every API request to ensure that retries don't result in double-charging your clients.
Leverage Antigravity to periodically audit your API usage to identify unused endpoints that are bloating your bundle size.
Store your pricing tiers in a remote config file rather than hardcoding them, allowing you to change prices without redeploying code.
content.next_steps: Once your billing service is live, integrate an analytics dashboard to track MRR and conversion rates using the same AI-assisted workflow.
content.conversion_block: Stop wasting time on manual API integrations and start building at scale with the tools the pros use. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
